source_pr.title: *: refine join result generator to return MaxChunkSize chunk
source_pr.body: Refine join result generator to return at most MaxChunkSize chunk every time. As the diagram shows, this commit only cost 1/4 memory usage when execute a Cartesian sql(as the 18:26~18:38 part shows), and save almost 47% memory usage on my machine when run tpch5. PTAL @zz-jason @coocood

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted results from joins/subqueries without an explicit ORDER BY, so row order could change with executor implementation details (e.g., join result generation and chunking). This PR stabilizes the assertions by sorting results (e.g., `result.Sort().Check(...)`) before comparing, making the checks deterministic.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: files: executor/builder.go, executor/index_lookup_join.go, executor/join.go, executor/join_result_generators.go, executor/join_test.go, executor/merge_join.go, util/chunk/iterator.go, util/chunk/iterator_test.go, tidb.go, executor/show_test.go, expression/integration_test.go, mysql/const_test.go, util/testkit/testkit.go
